subject: UX & Research
title: The Paradox of Choice
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Paradox of Choice.

hook.text: A grocery store offered 24 jam samples. Another day, just 6. The 6-jar display sold 10x more jam.

buildup.text: Sheena Iyengar's 2000 study showed that more options lead to fewer purchases and more regret.

discovery.text: Too many choices cause decision paralysis. The brain, overwhelmed, picks nothing to avoid picking wrong.

twist.text: Netflix has 15,000+ titles. They combat choice paralysis with personalized rows showing just 30-40 titles.

climax.text: Reduce options, add defaults, and curate. Help users choose quickly rather than giving them everything.

punchline.text: More choices, fewer decisions. Curate, don't overwhelm.

quiz.question: What happened in the jam study when 24 options were offered?
options: People bought much less jam compared to when only 6 were offered
Customers preferred the variety and bought more
